title: melicope major
intro: Melicope major, also known as the Hawaiian neem or simply neem, is a plant species native to Hawaii, known for its distinctive yellow flowers and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Hawaiian and Polynesian cultures, where its leaves and bark have been employed to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Melicope major is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds and infections. Herbal preparations of Melicope major, such as infusions and salves, can be used to address a range of health issues, including skin irritations and fungal infections.
